name: Melee Attack
description: A close-range attack that uses a hitbox, weapon arc, overlap volume, or trace near the attacker.
design_notes: Melee attacks are built from timing, reach, facing, collision, recovery, and hit reaction. A useful melee design defines startup, active frames, cancel rules, target filtering, and how the game communicates range. Weapon feel comes from hit stop, sound, camera response, and enemy reaction as much as damage values.
design_purpose: Give the player a direct close-range way to apply pressure or defeat enemies.
Create spacing decisions through reach, startup, recovery, and hit stun.
Support combat identity through weapon arcs, combo links, and target reactions.
player_fantasy: The player feels physically connected to the fight, reading distance and timing to land decisive hits.

parameters.name: melee_startup_frames
parameters.description: Frames before the attack can hit.

parameters.name: melee_active_frames
parameters.description: Frames during which the hitbox or trace can connect.

parameters.name: melee_recovery_frames
parameters.description: Frames before the attacker can take the next full action.

parameters.name: hitbox_forward_extent
parameters.description: Reach of the attack in front of the actor.

parameters.name: hitbox_arc_degrees
parameters.description: Horizontal or vertical arc covered by the attack.

parameters.name: hit_stop_frames_on_connect
parameters.description: Brief freeze applied on hit for impact readability.

edge_cases: Fast movement during active frames should not let the hitbox skip through small targets.
One swing must define whether it can hit one target once, many targets once, or the same target repeatedly.
Attacking near walls should handle weapon clipping and blocked arcs consistently.
Animation cancel, parry, dodge, and hit stun must agree on when the attack is interruptible.
common_bugs: Damage is applied every frame of overlap because hit targets are not tracked per attack instance.
Hitboxes spawn from animated bones that update after physics, causing one-frame visual mismatch.
Attack range differs between left and right facing due to unmirrored offsets.
Combo input is accepted during hit stop but discarded when the simulation resumes.
balancing_notes: Long reach and low recovery make melee safe; slower recovery or commitment preserves enemy counterplay.
Hit stun, knockback, and cancel windows should be balanced together because they determine whether pressure loops are possible.
accessibility_notes: Use clear hit confirmation through impact sound, hit flash, controller vibration, or hit markers.
Avoid requiring hidden animation-frame knowledge for basic attacks; reserve tight cancels for advanced mastery.

implementation_notes.unity: Create an attack_instance_id when startup begins, open hitboxes only during active frames, and maintain a per-target hit set so each target is damaged once per swing unless metadata allows repeats.
implementation_notes.godot: Track attack_instance_id, active_frame_range, and already_hit target IDs on the actor. Evaluate Area2D/Area3D overlaps or shape casts during physics ticks and emit damage only through the combat resolver.
implementation_notes.unreal: Use montage notifies or ability tasks to mark active frames, but keep attack_instance_id and per-target hit tracking in the combat component. Use traces or overlap queries with server-authoritative damage.
implementation_notes.web: Resolve melee from attack metadata, actor transform, active frame, and hit shape. Cache hit target IDs per attack_instance_id before queuing damage and hit-confirm events.

relationships.reason: Melee attacks need target reaction windows so hits have readable impact and combo timing can resolve.
Melee attack instances provide the active-frame hits and hit-confirm metadata used by combo chains.
Parry rules need attack startup, active frames, and source ownership to avoid ambiguous counter timing.

example_games.title: Devil May Cry 5
example_games.note: Uses melee attacks with varied timing, arcs, and cancel routes.

example_games.title: Hades
example_games.note: Uses close-range attacks with strong hit feedback and enemy reactions.
